summary: Verified the new multi-image picker on the Tile Visualizer end-to-end. Backend pytest (test_visualizer_multi_image.py + test_visualizer_admin_rooms.py + test_visualizer_v3_polish.py + test_visualizer_room_editor.py) ran 25 passed + 1 skipped — exactly matches the stated baseline. Direct API checks against POST /api/visualizer/sessions confirm: tile.images is returned as a list of 10 URLs, image_index 0/1/999 reflect correctly in the response (999 clamped to 9), and GET /api/visualizer/sessions/{id} persists tile_images, tile_image_index, and tile_image == tile_images[idx]. Coming Soon regression: unauthenticated visitor on /visualizer AND /visualizer?preview=1 both correctly show the 'Tile Visualizer — coming soon' page with the waitlist form (no admin token in localStorage). Homepage customer-facing nav has NO 'Visualize' link (verified link list). Frontend admin flow: logged in as admin@test.com / admin123, /admin/visualizer renders the 'Preview as customer' button (data-testid 'visualizer-admin-preview-btn') visible. /visualizer?preview=1 with the admin token in localStorage renders the real visualizer (10 sample-room cards, tile-search input, tile-picker). Searched 'Onyx', clicked the 'Scottish Onyx Polished 60x120cm' tile card which displays a '+2' badge bottom-right indicating extra images. The 'Texture image · 3 available' card (data-testid 'visualizer-tile-image-strip') appeared with 3 button thumbnails (data-testid visualizer-tile-image-0/1/2). thumb-0 was highlighted by default (yellow ring + check icon). Clicked thumb-1 — it became highlighted, thumb-0 deselected, the counter updated to 'Image 2 of 3', and the auto-render useEffect fired exactly the expected sequence: POST /api/visualizer/sessions {image_index:1,tile_id:...,sample_room_id:...,surface_kind:'floor'} → POST /sessions/{id}/render {style:'fast'} → POST /sessions/{id}/quote. The 'Showing Scottish Onyx Polished 60x120cm on Modern Kitchen Floor' caption matched throughout. Photoreal render NOT triggered (per instruction to avoid fal.ai cost).

minor.issue: Response body's tile.image stays as the canonical first image even when image_index>0 — only session.tile_image (returned by GET /sessions/{id}) reflects the chosen index. Pytest test_session_returns_full_image_gallery only checks idx=0 so it passes; not a regression but minor inconsistency between live response and persisted doc. The frontend uses tile_images[imageIdx] anyway so the render is correct.

critical_code_review_comments: TileVisualizerPage.jsx auto-render useEffect correctly lists [imageIdx, tile?.id, room?.id, surfaceKind] as deps — clicking a thumb triggers a fresh session POST + fast render. No infinite-loop risk because imageIdx only changes from user input (or the reset effect when tile.id changes).

imageIdx reset effect (when tile.id changes) is essential to prevent stale index when switching from a 10-image tile to a single-image tile — verified: single-image tiles correctly hide the strip card (length<2 guard).

TilePicker passes the full normalised images array up to the page on selection — no accidental truncation to first image. Backend _normalise_tile_images dedupes (verified by 10 unique URLs in a single product).
Backend clamp logic (max(0, min(idx, len-1))) handles both negative and out-of-range — pytest covers 9999 case.
Minor UX nit (not a bug): the +N badge on the picker card renders only on hover-state contrast — readable on the white tile card backgrounds I tested. No accessibility concerns observed.

Pre-existing console warning unrelated to this iteration: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop. Check the render method of AdminDashboard' — flagged in earlier iterations, not introduced by this change.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (multi-image picker, Coming Soon regression, homepage-nav regression all verified)

seed_data_creation: None — used existing 'Scottish Onyx Polished 60x120cm' product (10 images in DB) for backend probes and the same tile (3 displayed in picker after admin-side filtering) for the UI walk-through.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-image picker is fully shipped and verified. Backend image_index clamping works, session doc persists tile_image/tile_image_index/tile_images, and the frontend strip card + thumb buttons + auto-render are wired correctly. Coming Soon page still gates unauthenticated visitors (including ?preview=1). The earlier May 3 fix (no 'Visualize' link in customer nav) still holds. Photoreal render path was intentionally NOT exercised to avoid fal.ai charges — main agent can self-test that separately if desired. The minor backend inconsistency (response body's tile.image staying as canonical primary while session doc respects the chosen index) is harmless given the frontend reads tile_images[imageIdx] for its overlay caption.
